violations.text: Can opener blade was observed in poor repair. PIC took out of service to replace blade. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items were taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Pork, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Chicken was observed held at 55 degrees Fahrenheit, in a double-stacked pan in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

Establishment is vacuum packing cooked meats without variance approval. Vaccuum packaged foods were disposed. Procedure to apply for variance provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Inspector observed, confirmed by PIC, that refuse company placed receptacles in enclosure with covers open and against the rear wall of the enclosure. Staff are unable to close receptacles. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2025

The doors to the outside, located at the main entrance, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2024

A staff member was observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. PIC obtained a hair restraint for staff during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

The outside dumpsters are not covered. Inspector observed that the dumpsters were pushed against the wall, making it impossible for staff to close. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024

Inspector observed excessive boxes of marketing materials, empty boxes, and other materials in the basement storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2024

The door to the outside, located at the back door, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024
Cases of pretzels were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in and kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024
The refuse area has an accumulation of trash and debris. Refuse areas must be maintained clean to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

The ledge along the stairwell to the basement has an accumulation of dust and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area, including cases of merchandise on the basement floors. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2024

The front guest doors and loading dock gate are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/27/2023
Prep table faucet and ice machine were observed leaking. System to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 05/27/2023
Refuse area was observed with trash, litter and broken glass. Areas for refuse, recyclables and returnables shall be maintained free of litter and clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2023
